food_subgroup: Steamed filled buns (baozi) with seafood and meat filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun class (FOODON_03302757). No specific code exists for chilli crab and pork filled steamed bun. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers steamed bread and buns as a category. No specific code exists for chilli crab pork bao. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific filled steamed bun product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 steamed buns, each approximately 60 g, totalling approximately 180 g, as sold at Din Tai Fung Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Bao (steamed filled bun) with meat and seafood filling — nutrient estimation based on HPB Singapore Foodo reference values for steamed bao with pork filling and USDA FoodData Central data for crab meat and chilli sauce components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) Foodo / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for chilli crab and pork steamed bao exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values estimated by combining HPB Foodo reference for steamed pork bao (char siu bao steamed, ~220 kcal/100g, protein ~8g, fat ~5g, carbohydrate ~34g) with adjustment for crab meat filling contribution and chilli sauce. The bun component dominates the macronutrient profile. Estimates are consistent with published dim sum nutritional analyses. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Steamed wheat-flour bun (baozi) — GI estimated by analogy to steamed white bread and dim sum bun references in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chilli crab and pork steamed bao. GI of 65 estimated by analogy to steamed white flour bao and similar dim sum steamed buns reported in the University of Sydney GI Database (steamed white bread GI ~71; dim sum steamed bun analogues ~60–68). A midpoint of 65 is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (65 × 33.5 × 1.80) / 100 = 39.3 g per 180 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100g; ~756 mg per 3-bun serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of these buns due to the high sodium content from the chilli crab sauce and seasoned pork filling, which can contribute substantially to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 65) and high glycemic load per serving (~39 g GL) from refined wheat flour bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume these buns in moderation and pair them with low-GI foods or non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response driven by the refined wheat flour bun component.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat and high sodium; crab meat provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the sodium and saturated fat content in these buns, while noting that the crab meat filling contributes modest amounts of heart-beneficial omega-3 fatty acids; portion control is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Chilli Crab and Pork Buns (辣蟹鲜肉包) are a Singaporean-Chinese dim sum creation that pays homage to Singapore's iconic national dish, chilli crab. Each bun consists of a soft, fluffy steamed wheat-flour dough wrapper — made with premium imported flour — encasing a savoury filling of crab meat and minced pork seasoned with a tomato-chilli sauce that mimics the flavour profile of Singapore chilli crab. The buns are steamed until the dough is pillowy and slightly glossy, with a tender, moist interior. The flavour is a balance of sweet, spicy, and umami notes from the crab and chilli sauce, complemented by the richness of the pork. Texturally, the bun exterior is soft and yielding while the filling is juicy and slightly saucy. This dish is a signature item at Din Tai Fung Singapore and represents a fusion of traditional Shanghainese baozi craftsmanship with a distinctly Singaporean flavour identity. Nutritionally, each 3-piece serving (~180 g) provides approximately 392 kcal, 16.6 g protein, 10.4 g fat, and 60.3 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is elevated (~756 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The medium glycemic index (~65) and high glycemic load per serving (~39) require portion awareness for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The crab meat contributes modest omega-3 fatty acids and zinc. Allergens include gluten, crustaceans, egg, and soy. This dish is not halal due to the pork filling.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for chilli crab and pork steamed bao exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by analogy to steamed pork bao references from HPB Foodo with adjustment for crab meat and chilli sauce components. GI is estimated by analogy to steamed white flour bun references in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Foodo steamed pork bao reference and USDA FoodData Central crab meat data; no exact match exists for this specific product. Serving size of 180 g (3 × 60 g buns) is based on the product description (3 pieces) and typical dim sum bun weights. Sodium content is estimated and may vary significantly by restaurant preparation. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. This product is not halal due to pork content. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on steamed white flour bun references from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Glycemic load per serving = (65 × 33.5 × 1.80) / 100 = 39.3.
